assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling northbound in the left lane at about 60 km/h.
Vehicle was driving northbound in the left lane.
The car ahead drifted into the subject vehicle's lane.
The front of the subject vehicle struck the rear of the car ahead (front-to-rear impact).
There was one impact between the subject vehicle and the car ahead.
The driver braked just before the collision.
The car ahead drifted into the subject vehicle's lane before the collision occurred.
No explicit statement of severity; collision implied but severity unspecified.

verdicts.evidence: The statement claims the vehicle was traveling at about [60 km/h](T1) northbound, but the recorded speed values show a pre-impact speed range with a first_value of [121.0 km/h](T2) at t=[-5.0 s](T1) and a last_value of [111.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T1), which contradicts the claimed [60 km/h](T1).

The claim that the vehicle was driving northbound in the left lane is about heading and lane position; telemetry provides speed and steering but does not contain a heading or lane-level GPS/path record in the available channels, so lane and cardinal direction are [unverifiable](T1).
Whether the lead car drifted into V2's lane is a relative-trajectory / lane-position fact; the telemetry contains only V2 sensor channels and no external object tracks, so this is [unverifiable](T1).

EDR summary labels the event as [Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)](T1), and the crash-pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V with a peak signed value of [−81.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.16 s](T2), consistent with the subject vehicle experiencing a front-to-rear (frontal) impact with the other vehicle.

The crash-pulse statistics show a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak with magnitude [81.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.16 s](T2) and the detector returned one CISS impact event for delta-V_long (magnitude [81.0 km/h](T3)), consistent with one impact recorded between vehicles.
Brake channel transitions show a brake application at t=[-0.5 s](T2) (transition 0→1) and the detector reported a [brake application at t=-0.5 s](T3), indicating the driver braked just before impact.

Sequence claim that the lead car drifted into V2's lane before collision requires external object motion or lane-position data; available telemetry is limited to V2 sensors and cannot determine the other vehicle's prior motion, so this is [unverifiable](T1).
The brake channel shows a transition to ON at t=[-0.5 s](T2) and the impact occurs at t=[0 s](T1), so braking occurred before the impact.

The narrative states no explicit severity; telemetry shows a large longitudinal delta-V peak of [81.0 km/h](T2) which confirms a collision occurred but does not contradict that severity was unspecified in the narrative.

result.rationale: Telemetry records a clear frontal crash with a large longitudinal delta-V of [81.0 km/h](T2), a brake application at [−0.5 s](T2) before impact, and no lane/heading data to verify lane or other-vehicle maneuvers, so assertions about speed and other-vehicle motion are unverifiable or contradicted while impact, impact count, and braking timing are supported.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
